jokes.text: Do you really love the overzealous studio audience who will applaud at anything? Here in the greatest city on earth, New York City, baby? Whassup?
jokes.setup: Jack questioning whether people really love live TV
jokes.punchline: Immediately demonstrates his point with overzealous audience enthusiasm
jokes.explanation: Self-aware meta humor that immediately proves its own point - classic 30 Rock's smart comedy about TV itself

jokes.text: From now on, you write and shoot the whole season in two weeks like 'Wheel of fortune' or FOX news.
jokes.punchline: Comparing scripted game show to news network implies FOX news is equally fake
jokes.explanation: Brilliant political satire disguised as throwaway comparison - peak 30 Rock wit with perfect character voice

jokes.text: Best sweet 16 ever. I'm going to lose my virginity in nine years.
jokes.setup: Reminiscing about supposedly great Sweet 16 party
jokes.punchline: Reveals pathetically late virginity loss timeline from a 16-year-old's perspective
jokes.explanation: Perfect Liz Lemon character moment - captures her eternal awkwardness and oversharing with mathematical precision

jokes.text: There's a bathroom in here you can use, Cabletown coworker Kim Kardashian.
jokes.setup: Liz talking about live TV spontaneity
jokes.punchline: Completely random celebrity cameo acknowledgment proving nothing spontaneous happens
jokes.explanation: Decent live TV meta joke but relies on celebrity name-drop rather than deeper wit

jokes.text: I want to go to there.
jokes.punchline: Liz's signature grammatically incorrect phrase
jokes.explanation: Classic callback that works because it's perfectly timed character moment even if not original

jokes.text: But unlike the wildfires I've started, this one doesn't sexually arouse me.
jokes.punchline: Casually reveals arson and sexual deviance as character traits
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tracy Jordan insanity - escalates from simple metaphor to disturbing confession with brilliant economy

jokes.text: An important me-nouncement.
jokes.punchline: Self-absorbed portmanteau of 'me' and 'announcement'
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that perfectly captures Jenna's narcissism in a single made-up word

jokes.text: You didn't let me finish. S.
jokes.punchline: The interruption prevented her from saying the final 'S' of 'TGS'
jokes.explanation: Brilliant misdirection with perfect economy - the entire joke is one letter, maximum funny per word

jokes.text: I've been working on my reaction since I was 3. Aaah!
jokes.setup: Jenna wants to get engaged on live TV
jokes.punchline: Reveals decades of preparation for fake surprise reaction with demonstration
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jenna moment - her narcissism extends to rehearsing supposedly spontaneous emotional reactions

jokes.text: Oh, my god. He's going to die in there.
jokes.punchline: Liz's genuine concern for his life from overexposure
jokes.explanation: Solid character moment playing on Tracy's impulse control issues and internet addiction fears

jokes.text: '12 angry men' is preposterous Kenneth. 11 decent Americans are swayed by Jane Fonda's father?
jokes.setup: Kenneth referencing the classic jury drama
jokes.punchline: Jack reduces Henry Fonda to just 'Jane Fonda's father' and questions American justice
jokes.explanation: Classic Jack - dismissing American institutions with casual cultural references and business logic

jokes.text: Next stop, Hollywood, Florida to get the car from my mom. Next stop, California, pizza kitchen. And tell my old boss, to suck it. Next stop, Tinseltown, because Christmas decorations are really cheap this time of year.
jokes.setup: Hazel planning her path to Hollywood stardom
jokes.punchline: Completely confused geography mixed with mundane errands and discount shopping
jokes.explanation: Good character comedy showing Hazel's delusion through hilariously practical concerns

jokes.text: Made with milk, is the cheese that won World War II. Don't ask how.
jokes.punchline: Nonsensical WWII victory claim with mysterious disclaimer
jokes.explanation: Brilliant parody of vintage advertising's absurd claims with perfect mysterious ending

jokes.text: One of these days, Doris, I'm going to take a shot and blam! Blow your face off.
jokes.punchline: Escalates Honeymooners-style threats to actual murder
jokes.explanation: Dark subversion of classic sitcom tropes - turns Jackie Gleason's empty threats into genuine menace

jokes.text: I'm having a heart attack, too. My marriage is a sham. I got syphilis from Orson Wells.
jokes.setup: Husband having a heart attack on live TV
jokes.punchline: Escalates to personal confessions ending with bizarre celebrity STD
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation from medical emergency to marital problems to absurd celebrity connection

jokes.text: I'm doctor Harold Spaceman. I'm known in the industry as the 'Gay doctor', because I always have a smile on my face. Because I had homosexual lovers.
jokes.punchline: Explains nickname innocently then immediately contradicts with literal truth
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dr. Spaceman moment - medical authority figure with zero filter and complete honesty about inappropriate behavior

jokes.text: Your baby's lungs need refreshing nicotine for science reasons. And his growing bones need tar to hold them together.
jokes.punchline: Completely backwards medical advice delivered with scientific authority
jokes.explanation: Dark satire of historical tobacco marketing with Dr. Spaceman's characteristic medical incompetence

jokes.text: Nine out of ten doctors surveyed said, You have a lot of nerve calling here Spaceman, after what you've tried on my nephew.
jokes.punchline: Survey response is actually angry personal attack about past misconduct
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion of medical commercial tropes - turns statistical authority into personal condemnation

jokes.text: Thank you. Just portion control and exercise.
jokes.punchline: Her automatic response to any compliment as if it's about weight loss
jokes.explanation: Good character callback showing Jenna's self-absorption and body image obsession

jokes.text: Liza Minnelli's baby tooth surrounded by rubies that passed through a terrified Michael Kors.
jokes.punchline: Grotesque combination of celebrity body parts and designer victimization
jokes.explanation: Perfectly bizarre Paul description mixing celebrity worship with disturbing imagery

jokes.text: Rick Santorum is right Jenna. Marriage is a sacred union between a man and a woman.
jokes.punchline: Drag queen character citing conservative politician about traditional marriage
jokes.explanation: Brilliant irony of drag queen invoking conservative family values - perfect character contradiction

jokes.text: according to Roe v. Dwyane Wade, I have a right to choose.
jokes.punchline: Confuses landmark abortion case with basketball player
jokes.explanation: Classic Jenna malapropism showing her combination of self-importance and ignorance

jokes.text: I may have a dress and a wig and a gynecologist, but I am the man.
jokes.punchline: Lists increasingly feminine attributes while claiming manliness
jokes.explanation: Perfect Paul character moment - masculine posturing while listing feminine medical needs

jokes.text: Oh, I had a lobster role and two yogurts.
jokes.setup: Discussing why Lutz threw up when nervous
jokes.punchline: Misunderstands 'lobster roll' as 'lobster role' plus odd meal combination
jokes.explanation: Decent Lutz moment showing his confusion and poor dietary choices

jokes.text: I slept like a baby last night. I woke up crying with a boob in my mouth.
jokes.punchline: Takes 'like a baby' literally with adult implications
jokes.explanation: Classic setup/punchline structure taking familiar phrase to logical but inappropriate conclusion

jokes.text: I can see it inside your mouth.
jokes.punchline: Joey's inappropriate observation during performance
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy showing Joey's complete lack of professionalism and social awareness

jokes.text: I can see it inside your mouth.
jokes.punchline: Joey makes inappropriate observation mid-performance
jokes.explanation: Uncomfortable interruption showing Joey's unprofessional behavior and poor timing

jokes.text: Oh, I just burped up some clams casino which is weird because I didn't even have any.
jokes.punchline: Mysterious appearance of specific food he never ate
jokes.explanation: Weird Joey moment that's funny for its complete randomness and specificity

jokes.text: because they thought two black people on the same show would make the audience nervous. A rule NBC still uses today.
jokes.punchline: Reveals this racist policy continues in present day
jokes.explanation: Brutal self-aware criticism of network television's ongoing diversity problems delivered through Kenneth's innocence

jokes.text: Oh, hell no. I am not doing this.
jokes.setup: Black actor asked to perform racist character
jokes.punchline: Immediate refusal breaks character and scene
jokes.explanation: Powerful moment where actor's dignity overcomes contractual obligations - comedy from justified rebellion

jokes.text: I was a tuskegee airman. Zip-a-Dee-goo-goo.
jokes.setup: Actor asserting his war hero credentials
jokes.punchline: Immediately forced to say ridiculous racist nonsense
jokes.explanation: Devastating juxtaposition of American heroism with entertainment industry racism - peak uncomfortable comedy

jokes.text: Are you telling me the mayor of your hometown is a car?
jokes.setup: Kenneth saying cars replaced horses in his town
jokes.punchline: Jack's logical but absurd interpretation of Kenneth's statement
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist moment showing Jack's logical mind creating surreal scenarios from Kenneth's country background

jokes.text: Hello. I'm test tube adult doctor Leo Speceman.
jokes.punchline: 'Test tube adult' instead of 'test tube baby' doctor
jokes.explanation: Good Dr. Spaceman medical confusion showing his backward understanding of procedures

jokes.text: Then use the rectum you have instead of a mouth to say something like 'wrulgh' or 'thunghth'
jokes.setup: Medical commercial for birth defect victims
jokes.punchline: Grotesque medical descriptions with made-up sounds
jokes.explanation: Darkly absurd Dr. Spaceman moment combining medical authority with completely inappropriate descriptions

jokes.text: It's my period. It's a-gonna blow.
jokes.setup: Liz making excuse to leave Kenneth's meeting
jokes.punchline: Overshares menstrual information with explosive metaphor
jokes.explanation: Classic Liz oversharing with characteristically awkward phrasing and TMI content

jokes.text: We both know you're not due for another nine days.
jokes.setup: Liz claiming to have her period
jokes.punchline: Kenneth somehow knows her menstrual schedule
jokes.explanation: Perfectly creepy Kenneth moment - his inappropriate knowledge creates maximum discomfort

jokes.text: What do you get when you cross a lady driver with a with a Vietcong? A hundreed thousands senseless days.
jokes.punchline: Combines sexist and racist stereotypes with Vietnam War reference
jokes.explanation: Uncomfortable period-appropriate humor showing how offensive past entertainment was

jokes.text: I'm sorry, sweetheart, could you move out of the way of our camera? We need to talk to our new reporter there, Jamie Garnett.
jokes.setup: News anchors looking for their reporter at NASA
jokes.punchline: Dismisses actual reporter because she's a woman
jokes.explanation: Sharp satire of 1960s workplace sexism in journalism - cringe comedy from historical accuracy

jokes.text: that woman who stole his microphone went on to become a wife.
jokes.setup: Kenneth wrapping up the historical anecdote
jokes.punchline: Reduces accomplished reporter's entire future to marital status
jokes.explanation: Brilliant culmination of sexism theme - Kenneth's innocent delivery makes the historical dismissal even more cutting

jokes.text: This is New York state bitch, anyone can marry anything now.
jokes.setup: Hazel offering to marry Jenna for TV
jokes.punchline: Misunderstands marriage equality laws as complete legal chaos
jokes.explanation: Good Hazel moment showing her misunderstanding of laws combined with opportunistic scheming

jokes.text: Ow, my coccyx! It's not funny. Coccyx is the scientific term for your butt bone.
jokes.punchline: Defensive explanation that 'coccyx' is medical not dirty
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy moment with Tracy's characteristic need to educate people after embarrassment

jokes.text: Hello, I'd like to speak to General Electric. I was a nurse in the war and I met him when he was just a colonel.
jokes.setup: Tracy making prank call to GE
jokes.punchline: Treats corporate name as actual military person she dated
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tracy absurdist moment - treating corporation as personal romantic history with military progression

jokes.text: Daddy, it's your son, Toby Electric.
jokes.setup: Continuing the General Electric family fantasy
jokes.punchline: Creates fake child with corporate surname
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation of the General Electric bit - adds generational depth to absurd corporate personification

jokes.text: Yeah, except your mom last night.
jokes.setup: Jack declaring no one jokes about GE
jokes.punchline: Classic 'your mom' comeback
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective - Tracy using juvenile comeback against corporate executive creates good character moment

jokes.text: I will roast you alive in an oven I design myself, using two, no, three kinds of heat.
jokes.punchline: Oddly specific threat escalated with engineering precision
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jack moment - applies his corporate engineering mind to personal revenge fantasy with escalating technical detail

jokes.text: you should do it with the upcoming Warner Brothers movie, 'Rock of ages,' based on the hit Broadway musical. Rocking a theater near you June 15th. Tom Cruise sings.
jokes.punchline: Delivers complete movie advertisement mid-conversation
jokes.explanation: Good meta joke about product placement by actually doing obvious product placement

jokes.text: 'Old' being an acronym for oxylaprodexatrin, a hallucinogenic plant extract that makes sex terrifying.
jokes.setup: Paul saying he wants to grow old with Jenna
jokes.punchline: Creates fake drug acronym with disturbing sexual effects
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Paul moment - turns romantic cliche into elaborate drug reference with perfect specificity

jokes.text: No, no, a million times no. Not like this. I was wrong. You were right.
jokes.setup: Paul proposing on live TV as requested
jokes.punchline: Jenna rejects exactly what she demanded
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jenna character development - finally choosing love over publicity but still dramatically

jokes.text: Wow. Wow, 2.5 million people just saw that. And now 14 million people saw it.
jokes.punchline: Audience numbers growing in real-time as she speaks
jokes.explanation: Good meta commentary on viral moments and social media acceleration of embarrassment
